title: Unlocking Direct Python Access to MetaTrader 5 Market Streams
subtitle: mt5.initialize() Connection, Parquet Storage, Incremental Bar Updates, 72.88% Win Rate Deployment
author: Christian Benjamin (lynnchris)
verdict: LOW-MEDIUM EXTRACT for us — we already use Python-native MT5 connection via our BrokerDataAdapter. The z_spike feature (first difference / 20-bar rolling std) and EMA envelope (EMA×0.997, EMA×1.003) are the new additions worth extracting.

connection_pattern.storage: Parquet with zstandard compression — efficient for large bar histories
connection_pattern.incremental: On each poll: fetch last N bars, deduplicate by time, append to Parquet

keep: z_spike: first difference normalized by 20-bar rolling std = standard normal score of price move
EMA envelope bands: EMA ± 0.3% as dynamic S/R brackets
Parquet + zstandard for efficient large bar history storage
discard: MT5 connection setup — we already have BrokerDataAdapter
improve: z_spike is a normalized velocity measure — superior to our (close-open)/ATR for detecting extreme moves
Add z_spike as VWV feature: z_spike > 2.0 = significant move (2 standard deviations)
EMA envelope could define micro-zones: price touching env_low = potential bounce level

z_spike_vs_vwv.current_vwv: (close - open) / ATR — measures body energy relative to ATR
z_spike_vs_vwv.z_spike: first_diff / rolling_std(20) — measures bar-to-bar move in standard deviations
z_spike_vs_vwv.difference: VWV measures candle body momentum. z_spike measures price change magnitude vs recent volatility. z_spike is more sensitive to sudden directional moves; VWV is better for sustained momentum.
z_spike_vs_vwv.use_both: z_spike for spike detection (Crash/Boom). VWV for trend momentum (Forex).
